name: Certutil Initiated Connection
notes:
This rule detects a network connection intitiated by the certutil.exe tool. Attackers can abuse `certutil.exe` to download malware or offensive security tools.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: frack113, Florian Roth
SIGMA Title: Certutil Initiated Connection
SIGMA Description: Detects a network connection intitiated by the certutil.exe tool. Attackers can abuse `certutil.exe` to download malware or offensive security tools.


Confidence: HIGH

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following Access.ACL Permit
when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") MATCHES 'certutil\.exe' AQL filter query
when the event matches Initiated (custom) is any of true
when the event matches Destination Port is any of [80 or 443 or 135 or 445]
TD_list: APPLY Certutil Initiated Connection on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Access.ACL Permit
AND when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") MATCHES 'certutil\.exe' AQL filter query
AND when the event matches Initiated (custom) is any of true
AND when the event matches Destination Port is any of [80 or 443 or 135 or 445]
RED_list: Event description: Detects a network connection intitiated by the certutil.exe tool. Attackers can abuse `certutil.exe` to download malware or offensive security tools.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
